name: KrillApp.Server.Pin
title: GPIO Pin
description: Controls a GPIO pin on a Raspberry Pi via Pi4J. Supports output mode (set HIGH/LOW), input mode (read), and PWM mode. When invoked in output mode, sets the pin state; nodes observing the Pin as a source are then invoked. Registers and configures the pin with Pi4J on creation or edit. Allows configuring startup and shutdown states.
shortDescription: Controls a Raspberry Pi GPIO pin with configurable mode, state, and startup/shutdown behavior.
llmPurpose: Controls a physical GPIO pin on a Raspberry Pi, setting it HIGH or LOW, reading its state, or configuring PWM output.
llmBehavior: registers pin with Pi4J on creation
sets pin HIGH or LOW in output mode when invoked
applies the cascading verb (EXECUTE acts normally, RESET stands down)
reads pin state in input mode
on state change, nodes observing the Pin as a source are invoked
is a data-source type — its state is readable as an input via its snapshot
configures startup and shutdown pin states
llmOutputs: observers invoked on state change; pin state readable from its snapshot
llmCreationHints: Create a Pin node to control a specific GPIO pin on a Raspberry Pi. Requires a pin number (BCM address) and mode (OUT for output, IN for input). Configure initial and shutdown states as needed. To drive the pin from automation, list the controlling node (Button, LogicGate, trigger) in the Pin's sources with SOURCE_INVOKED.

llmConnectionHints.role: leaf target node that directly controls physical hardware
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Observer-side: list the controller (Button, LogicGate, trigger) in sources with SOURCE_INVOKED; the Pin applies the cascading verb (EXECUTE/RESET). It is also a data-source type — a DataPoint records its state by observing it via its own sources+inputs.

llmExamples: Set this relay pin to HIGH at startup and LOW at shutdown.
Toggle this LED on and off.
Read the state of this digital input pin.
